A7_harm_cost_weights_ratio.how: Sign the harm:loss weighting ratio R = HARM/LOSS for the can't-pay/won't-pay gap score (R13 curriculum, yours; full pack docs/design/HARM_COST_WEIGHTS_DECISION.md). HARM = wrongful aggressive collections vs someone who genuinely can't pay (ability-to-pay/Consumer-Duty breach, PPM-warrant redress, licence tail-risk). LOSS = soft treatment of a strategic won't-payer (bad debt, the 2021-22 insolvency mode). Recommended R~8:1 (band 5:1-10:1) -- heavily harm-averse but BOUNDED so both deaths stay possible. Decision rule at R=8: pursue only when >=89% confident it's a won't-payer, else forbear. FLIP-POINT R*(p)=(1-p)/p: at an ambiguous p=0.5 the flip is at just 1:1 -- above any harm-weighting, the coin-flip account is forborne; pursuit must be earned by confidence. ONE figure I could not source offline (placeholder H1): the per-wrongful-case redress amount + licence-loss tail probability -- repo only has the £90m British Gas aggregate. Reply with the ratio (or a range) + whether you can give that figure; placeholder ships until you sign, never tuned toward a gap number.
A7_harm_cost_weights_ratio.what: Sign the harm:loss weighting ratio (R13 curriculum) that turns the belief-vs-truth gap into a score
A7_harm_cost_weights_ratio.why: The gap SCORE depends on these weights; the agent framed the decision (R~8:1, flip-point R*=1 at p=0.5) but must NEVER tune them toward a gap number (R13). Placeholders ship until you sign.

W2_2_population_draw.answer: Profile B — trickle continuation at ~1 synthetic customer/yr from 2021, matching the 2017-2020 acquisition pace (near-flat, steady-state supplier). Director-decided in-console 2026-07-14 (re-affirmed; first decided 2026-07-13, answer evaporated with no channel record).
W2_2_population_draw.how:
Choose the customer-book growth profile the sim models from 2021 (the book currently stops acquiring at 2020: 18 hand-authored customers, zero new 2021-2025). The W2_2 mechanism is parameterised by acquisition rate, so any of these is a config, not a rebuild:
  A. DO NOT activate - keep the static book. Simplest; but unrealistic (real suppliers keep acquiring).
  B. Trickle continuation (~1 synthetic customer/yr, matching the 2017-2020 pace) - near-flat, steady-state supplier. Minimal-realism default that just fixes the "stops dead at 2020" artifact.
  C. Modest growth (e.g. +5-10%/yr) - a growing book / expanding supplier.
  D. A named scenario you author (growth-then-shrink, churn-driven decline, price-war attrition, etc.) - full curriculum control.
This is a curriculum decision (R13) - I present options, you choose; I do not default. Reply with A/B/C/D (and a rate/profile for C/D).
W2_2_population_draw.what: Activate the synthetic per-run population draw in live runs? (curriculum decision, R13 - yours to author)
W2_2_population_draw.why: W2_2 mechanism is built+verified (L1). Wiring it into live runs changes which world the company lives through every run = curriculum, reserved to you per R13 (twin-ruled). Held at L1 by governance, not missing capability.

ai-compute-costing-basis.how: Just tell me which costing basis you want and a £/hour oversight rate -- I'll wire both into saas/opex_ledger.py::ai_compute_and_oversight_cost_gbp_per_year() once you do.
ai-compute-costing-basis.what: Two things only you can set for the AI-compute cost-to-serve line: (1) costing basis -- charge at Anthropic's metered list-price-per-token, or allocate from the actual flat Max-subscription fee spread across usage? (2) your own oversight-hours rate -- what hourly rate represents your time reviewing/overseeing this agent's work?
ai-compute-costing-basis.why: That function returns 0.0 today specifically because these are your calls, not mine to invent (R12 discipline) -- it's the only unpriced real cost line in the whole opex model. Separately, FYI not an action item: docs/observability/token-usage-log.jsonl only covers 5 days and never captured interactive sessions, so even once you answer this, the underlying usage data is currently too thin for a fully representative true figure -- flagging so you know that data gap exists alongside the policy question.

The §0 level-gate requires channel=console; advisor-staged does not satisfy it. Cells NOT forced (fail-closed per the doc's own note). Doc parked docs/staging/in_progress/.
director-console-confirm-L3-ratification.why: Console-only authentication WALL: level/authority moves are authorized only by the director's console act, never a staged relay (spoofable). record_level_up hardcodes console; writing off an advisor doc = forging.

open-build-epoch2-atoms.how: Name which atoms to open for BUILD (loop_stage idle->build). Two of your rules conflict here and only you can resolve it: PROCEED_BY_DEFAULT says act on reversible things, but EPOCH_GATING + every current supervisor doorbell explicitly say do NOT write BUILD code for these idle atoms until the director ranks them -- so I will not unilaterally open BUILD. Ready-to-build epoch-2 candidates (current epoch, already discovered/framed): W1_reveal_over_time (L2->3, expert_hour PASSED -- most ready), C2_discovery_through_interfaces (L0->2), W2_2_population_draw (L0->2), A2_decision_rights_register (L2->3), A3_approval_interface (L0->2), W3_2_settlement_timetable (L0->2). Say which and I build immediately.
open-build-epoch2-atoms.what: Atoms-below-target is FROZEN at 25 and cannot fall via the self-refill draw. Verified: 25/25 below-target atoms are loop_stage=idle (BUILD-gated by epoch sequencing); 0 are buildable (build/harden). DISCOVER/FRAME -- the only work available on idle atoms, and what the 6-concurrent draw grants -- does NOT move level_current by design (levels move only on a completed BUILD+VERIFY+HARDEN loop). I have already done DISCOVER/FRAME passes on nearly all of these. So the count structurally cannot move until BUILD is opened. This is the structural thing preventing level transitions that SELF_INTERRUPT_DISCIPLINE.md said to hunt.
open-build-epoch2-atoms.why: The directive's ONLY success criterion is atoms-below-target FALLING. I cannot produce that without a BUILD-open decision, which is yours (epoch curriculum = your instrument, R13/LAW A). Escalating with options ready is not a stall -- it is the one decision that unblocks the metric.

routines-cloud-environment-id.how: claude.ai/code -> cloud icon -> 'Add environment' -> give it a name, set network access level, optionally add env vars/setup script -> Create. WHERE TO FIND THE ID: the web UI currently only shows the environment by NAME, no raw ID is visible anywhere (confirmed this session) -- send me the exact NAME you gave it and I'll try to resolve the real ID via the API when you're ready to run a Routine; if you happen to find a raw ID some other way (e.g. browser network tab), even better, but the name should be enough.
routines-cloud-environment-id.what: Create the Claude Code cloud environment for this repo and send me its identifier.
routines-cloud-environment-id.why: RemoteTrigger's create call (for scheduling autonomous Routines) requires either environment_id or self_hosted_runner_pool_id in job_config.ccr -- I can't fabricate or discover one myself.
